title: Mirror Image Theory
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 11, 2011 10:41 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: "mirror image" theory

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 12, 2011 8:22 am Reichu wrote: Wanted to ask you about this whole "mirror image" thing. I read your post earlier, and have heard you bring this up several more times, but I'm having trouble figuring out exactly what the "theory" is. Was it formally presented somewhere else? As is, the most I've been able to get out of it is, "NME subverts many elements of the original, and therefore... stuff ".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 12, 2011 3:16 pm This seems way too vague and picky-choosy, and too out-of-touch with the creative madness ultimately employed by helmsman Anno, to have any practical application, and it seems more like an exercise in conceit and futility than anything else. It feels especially unnecessary in light of the fact that the information in the available movies hasn't even been fully tapped for its predictive power.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed Jan 12, 2011 3:24 pm :P http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=9917&start=0

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Jan 12, 2011 3:30 pm Even beyond the fact that the production method doesn't seem to reveal any such intent, the mirror theory seems to fall apart when considering how many of the changes and additions in Rebuild have no analogue to the events in NGE at all: new characters, new political treaties, bases on the moon, space flights, new Evas, new powers, altered mythology, new symbolism, etc. qu4d wrote: [ images ]

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed Jan 12, 2011 3:40 pm @OP: Referring to this? http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mirror_matter

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 12, 2011 4:01 pm qu4d wrote: @OP: Referring to this? http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mirror_matter

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 12, 2011 6:19 pm I'm getting the feeling that "mirror theory" is just a restatement / corollary of something we've known for quite some time... NGE heavily employs a storytelling tactic that's basically, "you never see anything just once". Dialogue and visuals reverberate throughout the piece -- repetitions, variations on a theme... you might call some of it "mirroring" (though I personally dislike that term; too ambiguous). Some of this serves as prognostication (albeit a lot of it only obvious in retrospect), but it ties the narrative together any which way. NME, of course, gets to continue this tradition on an internal level, and on a meta level by referencing the previous incarnation of Eva . It seems to me that your theory boils down to some of these inter- Eva juxtapositions containing predictive power, in the same way that they can be predictive on an intra- Eva level. I'm not sure a "theory" really needs to be built out of this -- TBH, I don't think a word like "theory" really applies. (If it does, then "never just once" would be a "theory", too, and that's just... yeah.) I also think you shouldn't give this one element of the storytelling too much credit where attempting to predict the future is concerned. Also, +1 to what essel said. (Now how could I have forgotten that part..?)

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 12, 2011 7:10 pm @Reichu: You have just explained it better than I have so far. I indeed mean this meta-level application of such a "you never see it once" principle, although I would limit it to meaningful scenes for character development. However, I believe Anno (or whoever designs the storyboards and dialogue for Evangelion) is masterful at this literary mechanic, which is my main enjoyment of complex stories. I tend to view Evangelion as a literary masterpiece, and like picking it apart for deliberate foreshadowing, deliberately hidden themes, and deliberately obscure parallels with mythology, warships, or whatever. I emphasize the "deliberate" (or seemingly deliberate) part, because that is what sets it apart from grasping at straws. It is, of course, far too systematic for an organic story like Evangelion (NGE or Rebuild), especially considering the abundance of (cruel) irony that has its home in the deeper layers of the story, but I do believe the "mirror image theory" may lead to a few correct educated guesses (in addition to a lot of drivel). Also, I believe I have been rather conscientiously describing the "mirror image theory" as a "theory" with quotes attached, which is meant to indicate it as a pet theory, or at least my method of making only-slightly-founded speculations. Finally, I actually like the term "mirroring" for its ambiguity between "the same" and "opposite", because it allows for the playfulness inherent in telling a many-layered story. You could conceive of it as a patch to cover up the weakness of an inaccurate "theory", but the thing is that it is actually supposed to work on intuition. Hence the quotation marks. So in conclusion, what I refer to as "mirror image theory" is actually an intuitive tool for: - discovering thematic parallels between scenes and story events in published Evangelion material; - brainstorming over possible story developments in yet-to-be-published material. And I'm convinced it has its uses. EDIT: NGE Asuka makes cuter faces, therefore, NGE wins. I'm glad to have finally settled this once and for all in a way we can all surely agree on.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 12, 2011 7:35 pm Jorno: I hazard to suggest that we've probably been intuitively using the principles you want to pet-theory-ize to make guesses all this time. Ultimately, it's a tool for generating speculation, yes. But consider what's happened so far. An entire forum, using every tool at their disposal, could accurately predict almost nothing about 2.XX -- and, even then, a lot of the "success" was simply due to the "million monkeys at a typewriter" dynamic. You reeeeal-ly think you'll be able to take one of those tools and make accurate predictions about the next installment, which we already know will diverge even further from the familiar narrative into completely unknown territory? I'm willing to venture that your success rate will be about as good as anyone else's. Self-indulgent guessing games don't need to be "qualified" like you're doing. Let's just have fun biding time without pretensions of following some special prescribed future-telling method pioneered by Fan XXX.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 12, 2011 7:53 pm Reichu wrote: Jorno: I hazard to suggest that we've probably been intuitively using the principles you want to pet-theory-ize to make guesses all this time. Ultimately, it's a tool for generating speculation, yes. But consider what's happened so far. An entire forum, using every tool at their disposal, could accurately predict almost nothing about 2.XX -- and, even then, a lot of the "success" was simply due to the "million monkeys at a typewriter" dynamic. You reeeeal-ly think you'll be able to take one of those tools and make accurate predictions about the next installment, which we already know will diverge even further from the familiar narrative into completely unknown territory? I'm willing to venture that your success rate will be about as good as anyone else's. Self-indulgent guessing games don't need to be "qualified" like you're doing. Let's just have fun biding time without pretensions of following some special prescribed future-telling method pioneered by Fan XXX.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jan 13, 2011 2:24 pm I just want to chip in that I've never noticed how different Asuka's hair was between the two versions. It's really...I don't know, jarring?

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu Jan 13, 2011 2:29 pm AuraTwilight wrote: I just want to chip in that I've never noticed how different Asuka's hair was between the two versions. It's really...I don't know, jarring?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Jan 13, 2011 5:29 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: I think most of it is in the colouring. The series of images from NGE appear to be taken on a darker brightness setting than those from Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jan 15, 2011 12:02 pm ...Rei DOES say "I am not a puppet" in the original NGE Elevator scene, way before EoE. At this point, she's not saying that she'd kill herself for Gendo because she follows any order (see eps 16 and 19), but because of what Gendo means to her.
